key_points: Dorm experience is memorable but universally described as something to survive
Will Vill, Kitt, Farrand all functional — roommate lottery matters more than building
Farrand being gutted for a full year starting 2026, displacing students
key_quotes: I had the best time one could possibly have there. Would I want to do it again? No.
Your experience in the dorms will be made up more by your roommates and neighbors than any single attribute of the hall itself.
Kitt Central is full of nerds who only study and go to sleep by 10pm

key_points: Party scene is real and dominant — non-drinkers report feeling excluded
The 'hippie Boulder vibe' is largely gone, priced out to Nederland and nearby towns
Nightlife reportedly declining year-over-year since pre-COVID peak
key_quotes: I toured pre Coach Prime so I was expecting a much different vibe — does anyone have suggestions to meet people that don't do coke to stay skinny?
The hippies were priced out.
I can't help but think there's a lot less [nightlife] than there used to be.

key_points: Farrand Market disappearing for a full year during dorm renovations
No significant student praise for dining options in available data
Off-campus food options limited but growing (new food trucks noted)
key_quotes: Farrand will be completely gutted starting next year — the alley and Farrand market will be gone for a full year.
New food truck in the parking lot behind the District — Indian food, smaller bites and vegetarian.

key_points: Multiple posts about loneliness and social isolation, especially freshman year
Drug use in dorms is real enough that students advise keeping Narcan on hand
Boulder's intense fitness culture can be alienating for students who don't fit that mold
key_quotes: Get some Narcan to have on hand. Even if you don't use drugs, someone in the dorms inevitably does.
To be 100% transparent, Boulder is a brutal place to be obese.
I'm really introverted, aroace, overweight, never wanna drink/smoke or party — everyone is saying it's hard to find friends like me.

key_points: Strong merit scholarships exist — Presidential Scholarship ($55K) and Engineering merit are real
In-state cost (~$20K/year) seen as excellent value vs. comparable OOS schools
Pell Grant cancellation errors reported, with slow admin response over breaks
key_quotes: Comes with $55K Presidential Scholarship + $12K Engineering merit scholarship.
Boulder is $20k/year (in-state + scholarship). At the time of choosing I was confident in my decision.
I just got an email saying my Pell Grant was canceled even though it shows on my FAFSA that I still have it.

key_points: Strong programs in aerospace, physics, CS, and environmental sciences confirmed by students
Finance degree criticized as too easy with excessive grade handholding
AI/ChatGPT cheating is widespread in grad programs, undermining academic integrity
key_quotes: The finance degree feels like a joke — a lot of handholding to make sure no one can possibly fail.
Everybody uses ChatGPT for EVERYTHING. They use it to do all their homework, to write all their papers.
CU Boulder professor here — the school is excellent and many of our programs are nationally top-ranked.

key_points: University secretly signed $2.1M ChatGPT deal, blindsiding students and faculty for two months
Broke explicit 'Email for Life' promise to 320K+ alumni, then announced AI contract days later
Shared governance routinely bypassed — faculty and students excluded from major decisions
key_quotes: YALL CONSULTED NO ONE.
CU is all about the money. They need that cash for the football program, to tear down and replace something.
It is essentially free when you have a system scaled up like this. We pay them tens of thousands per year to get a degree and they can't maintain a simple email?

key_points: Campus and Boulder location are consistently praised as a major draw
WiFi is notoriously bad — 1-5 Mbps speeds, constant dropouts, students switching networks constantly
Boulder is expensive and has shifted from countercultural to wealthy/preppy demographic
key_quotes: Coloradoan here — sko buffs! The campus is the absolute prettiest and Boulder is the nicest town.
Why the actual fuck is the wifi so fucking awful. I can't open Canvas without aging another 6 months.
The hippies were priced out of Boulder a long time ago.

key_points: Economics grads reporting severe job market struggles — 5 of 6 classmates unable to find salaried work
Engineering and CS grads employable, especially with internship experience
Consulting careers accessible from CU Boulder with right hustle — alumni confirm this
key_quotes: Out of my classmates in the Economics department literally 5/6 people have been able to find salaried positions. Everything is no salary and commission based now.
I went to CU and was hired immediately after graduation into consulting. I also hired some great people out of CU into consulting jobs.
A lot less these days about what you know and instead who you know.

key_points: In-state tuition is genuinely competitive — widely cited as smart financial choice
OOS without scholarships is expensive and hard to justify over comparable in-state options
Administration priorities (AI contracts, football) signal money going away from students
key_quotes: Boulder is $20k/year in-state + scholarship. I wouldn't be graduating with any debt.
CU has been only about selling student loans and football tickets for decades now.
It's going to cost them future donations. I had hoped to donate to the school eventually. That's pretty much dead now.

key_points: Outdoor-focused, active, and beautiful — a genuine draw for nature-loving students
Student body skews white, wealthy, and fitness-obsessed — less diverse than advertised
Strong school spirit around football (Coach Prime era) but cultural homogeneity is real
key_quotes: I visited pre Coach Prime and was expecting a much different vibe.
Boulder, especially for engineering/CS, isn't a 'safety.' Boulder's an amazing school.
Go live your life to the fullest — you guys know how to make people smile.

key_points: **Administration Trust**: $2.1M AI deal signed secretly, Email for Life promise broken without warning
**Student Safety**: Suspicious student death ruled suicide despite blunt force trauma evidence; new missing-student law passed in response
**Campus Security**: Bomb threats, SWAT calls, active shooter false alarms at Norlin Library reported in recent years
key_quotes: It is absolutely asinine that her death was ruled a suicide, given the evidence presented.
CU EMERGENCY ALERT: Police investigating unconfirmed bomb threat. K-9 units are on scene.
Former CU Boulder student sentenced to 90 days in jail for campus sexual assault — '90 days is not enough.'

key_points: Library loans free Colorado State Park passes — massively underutilized perk
Physics and aerospace programs are nationally respected and surprisingly accessible
LGBTQ+ Spectrum housing wing offers gender-neutral dorms and dedicated community space
key_quotes: CU Boulder Libraries checks out free Colorado State Park passes.
CU Boulder is one of the top schools for physics — and I am majoring in it.
Take a look at the Spectrum community at CU Boulder — they've got a dedicated wing for LGBTQ students with gender neutral rooms and restrooms.
